tools.name: lessly_deploy
tools.summary: Build and deploy a git ref to a Lessly environment.
tools.description: Triggers a build of the specified git ref and promotes the resulting artifact to the target environment. Returns a deployment id you can poll with lessly_get_deployment or stream logs from with lessly_get_logs.

args.name: app
args.description: Slug of the Lessly app to deploy (matches the name in your workspace).

args.name: ref
args.description: Git ref — branch name, tag, or commit SHA. Defaults to the app's default branch.

args.name: environment
args.description: Target environment. Preview deploys get a unique URL; production replaces the live URL after the health check passes.

args.name: wait
args.description: If true, the tool returns only after the deployment reaches a terminal state (ready or failed). If false, returns as soon as the build is queued.

example.prompt: Deploy the main branch of acme-store to production and wait for it to go live.

tools.name: lessly_get_deployment
tools.summary: Fetch the status and metadata of a single deployment.
tools.description: Returns the current state (queued, building, deploying, ready, failed, canceled), the build duration, the commit metadata, and the URL of the deployment. Use this to poll a deployment kicked off without wait=true.

args.name: deployment_id
args.description: Id returned by lessly_deploy or lessly_list_deployments.

example.prompt: Check the status of deployment dpl_8x2k.

tools.name: lessly_list_deployments
tools.summary: List recent deployments for an app, newest first.
tools.description: Returns up to limit deployments for the given app, sorted by created_at descending. Filter by environment to scope the list (for example, only production deploys) or by status to find the last successful build.

args.name: app
args.description: Slug of the Lessly app.

args.name: environment
args.description: Restrict results to one environment.

args.name: status
args.description: Restrict results to one terminal or in-flight state.

args.name: limit
args.description: Maximum number of deployments to return. Capped at 100.

example.prompt: Show me the last 5 production deployments of acme-store.

tools.name: lessly_get_logs
tools.summary: Read build or runtime logs for a deployment.
tools.description: Returns log lines for the given deployment, scoped to either the build phase or the runtime worker. Supports follow=true for live tailing while a build is in progress.

args.name: deployment_id
args.description: Id of the deployment to read logs from.

args.name: source
args.description: Which log stream to read. build covers the build step; runtime covers requests served by the deployment.

args.name: since
args.description: ISO-8601 timestamp or relative duration like 15m or 2h. Lines older than this are dropped.

args.name: follow
args.description: Stream new lines as they arrive instead of returning a single snapshot.

args.name: limit
args.description: Maximum number of lines to return when follow is false. Capped at 1000.

example.prompt: Tail the runtime logs for deployment dpl_8x2k for the last 15 minutes.

tools.name: lessly_set_env
tools.summary: Create or update an environment variable on an app.
tools.description: Writes a single env var to the app. Marking secret=true encrypts the value at rest and redacts it in logs and tool output. The change applies on the next deployment unless redeploy=true is set.

args.name: app
args.description: Slug of the Lessly app.

args.name: key
args.description: Env var name. Must match /^[A-Z_][A-Z0-9_]*$/.

args.name: value
args.description: Value to store. Pass an empty string to set the var to empty; use lessly_list_envs and the workspace UI to delete.

args.name: environment
args.description: Which environments the var applies to.

args.name: secret
args.description: Encrypt at rest and redact in logs. Default true for keys ending in _KEY, _TOKEN, _SECRET, _PASSWORD.

args.name: redeploy
args.description: Trigger a redeploy of the current production deployment so the new value takes effect immediately.

example.prompt: Set STRIPE_SECRET_KEY on acme-store in production as a secret and redeploy.

tools.name: lessly_list_envs
tools.summary: List environment variables configured on an app.
tools.description: Returns the env vars for the given app and environment. Secret values are redacted; only the key, environment, and last-updated timestamp are returned for secrets.

args.name: app
args.description: Slug of the Lessly app.

args.name: environment
args.description: Scope the listing to one environment.

args.name: include_secrets
args.description: Include keys flagged as secret. Values remain redacted regardless of this flag.

example.prompt: List every env var on acme-store in production.

tools.name: lessly_add_domain
tools.summary: Attach a custom domain to an app.
tools.description: Registers a custom hostname for the app and returns the DNS records (CNAME or A) you need to add at your registrar. After DNS verification, Lessly provisions a TLS certificate automatically.

args.name: app
args.description: Slug of the Lessly app.

args.name: hostname
args.description: Fully-qualified hostname to attach, e.g. www.acme.com or acme.com.

args.name: environment
args.description: Which environment the domain serves. Preview deploys keep their auto-generated URLs.

args.name: redirect_apex_to_www
args.description: When hostname is an apex domain, also configure a 308 redirect from apex to www.

example.prompt: Point www.acme.com at the production deployment of acme-store.

tools.name: lessly_rollback
tools.summary: Promote a previous deployment back to production.
tools.description: Re-promotes a known-good deployment to the live URL. No rebuild happens — the existing artifact is re-pointed, so rollback completes in seconds. The current production deployment remains queryable via lessly_list_deployments.

args.name: app
args.description: Slug of the Lessly app.

args.name: deployment_id
args.description: Deployment to roll back to. If omitted, rolls back to the most recent ready deployment before the current production one.

args.name: reason
args.description: Free-text note attached to the rollback event for the audit log.

example.prompt: Roll acme-store production back to deployment dpl_7w1j because checkout is broken.
